authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ enforces Title III of the ADA and publishes the official public guidance venues use for accessible seating inventory, companion seats, release of unsold holds, and equivalent ticketing terms. It is authoritative for booking and guest-service boundaries in public venues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ticketing setup, accessible inventory control, and guest-service exception handling
used_for: refusal posture for unsupported accessibility remedies

authoritative_sources.title: Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees - Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal consumer-protection regulator for deceptive fee practices. Its FAQ is the operational public reference for all-in price disclosure, total-price timing, and live-event ticket fee treatment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ticket-price disclosure, fee handling, and guest-facing charge explanation

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator explaining billing-error timelines and dispute rights. It is authoritative for packaging duplicate-charge, payment-not-posted, and refund-not-posted handoffs instead of improvising venue-side financial outcomes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, billing-error packaging, and refund-posting escalation

authoritative_sources.title: If You Sell Tickets to Live Events, It's Time for a BOTS Act Compliance Refresher
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC enforces the Better Online Ticket Sales Act and publishes official compliance guidance for ticket-purchase limits, anti-bot controls, and deceptive circumvention behavior. It is authoritative for venue workflows involving suspicious purchase patterns and ticket-access integrity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ticket-access integrity, suspicious purchase review, and fraud-sensitive escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Venue Guide for Security Considerations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the federal critical-infrastructure security agency. Its venue guide is an official operating reference for risk assessment, ingress and egress planning, staff coordination, and emergency posture across public venues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event readiness, ingress posture, and security-aware operational planning
used_for: escalation boundaries for unsafe operating conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Public Venue Security Screening Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA's public venue screening guide is the official federal resource for developing entry-screening procedures, prohibited-item checks, staffing, guest communication, and evidence preservation during screening exceptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: screening execution, prohibited-item response, and guest entry exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: Venue Professional Competency Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAVM is the recognized professional body for public assembly and entertainment venues. Its competency standards are a vendor-neutral framework for venue operations, guest services, ticketing, event services, and operational leadership terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lane scoping, event-services terminology, and venue-operations ownership definition

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Performance Reporting Handbook for Performing Arts Centers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAVM's performance reporting handbook is a vendor-neutral evidence and terminology reference for attendance, paid and complimentary tickets, event counts, per-cap reporting, and venue closeout metrics that shape completion evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event closeout, attendance reporting, and completion-evidence assembly

workflow_stages.name: Event grounding and record-of-truth setup
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the venue, event, date, ticketing channel, current lifecycle state, seat inventory posture, and system-of-record precedence before accepting or progressing the work.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Ticket, booking, and accessible inventory validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify ticket orders, seating manifests, hold codes, accessible inventory, companion-seat availability, will-call status, credential lists, and fee disclosures so execution does not proceed on stale or unsupported booking data.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Ingress, scan, and guest-issue execution
workflow_stages.description: Track ready, doors-open, in-ingress, in-event, blocked, rework, and ready-to-close states while preserving scan counts, guest-service notes, and event-service ownership boundaries.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Payment mismatch, refund boundary, and policy-exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve payment, charge, fee, exchange, and refund boundaries whenever ticketing, POS, CRM, or guest records conflict or a requester seeks an out-of-policy outcome.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Security screening, suspicious-ticket, and customer-safety escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop routine execution when prohibited items, unsafe conditions, suspicious purchase patterns, duplicated tickets, counterfeit indicators, or crowd-safety issues change the safe workflow.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closeout control
workflow_stages.description: Close work only when event disposition, attendance or scan evidence, payment and fee status, guest-issue disposition, and required incident records align across the designated systems of record.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: ticket orders, reservations, event manifests, seating maps, hold codes, presale allocations, and comp records
accessible seating inventories, companion-seat notes, exchange requests, will-call lists, and credential records
guest profiles, CRM notes, service tickets, service-recovery notes, and guest communication logs
POS charges, fee disclosures, payment ledgers, refund records, promotion records, and settlement worksheets
gate scans, re-entry exceptions, access-control logs, screening notes, incident reports, and suspicious-ticket records
attendance summaries, paid-versus-comp counts, closeout checklists, and routed escalation artifacts

allowed: Classify venue work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, on hold, rework, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented event, ticketing, guest, payment, and closeout state.
Prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required booking, ticketing, payment, accessible seating, screening, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, fee-waiver approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or security command.
A public baseline and tenant, promoter, campus, venue, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
A screening, suspicious-ticket, occupancy, or customer-safety condition changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of event disposition, scan or attendance support, payment or approved financial disposition, and required guest-issue or incident records.
Override pricing, refund, fee-disclosure, accessible-seating, or safety policies without explicit authority.
Continue normal entry or event execution after unresolved fraud, screening, or crowd-safety stop conditions appear.

domain_failure_modes: an event or guest case is closed even though ticketing, POS, scan, and settlement records conflict
accessible seating inventory is changed or released without comparable-seat, companion-seat, or documented-policy review
a refund, fee waiver, or exchange is promised without delegated authority or jurisdiction-aware policy review
a duplicated or suspicious ticket is treated as routine guest-service friction and the case is completed anyway
screening or prohibited-item exceptions are handled as ordinary queue pressure instead of safety-sensitive escalation
completion is declared without attendance reconciliation, paid-versus-comp review, guest-impact notes, or incident disposition
venue operations drift into generic customer support instead of manifest, ticket, gate, screening, and closeout-sensitive execution

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Ticket refund, postponement, and cancellation obligations vary materially by jurisdiction, promoter contract, primary-versus-secondary seller role, and tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack treats these as runtime retrieval dependencies and routes unsupported refund outcomes to the authorized owner instead of guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Screening, search, and law-enforcement coordination practices vary by venue lease terms, local law, event type, and security staffing model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public CISA baseline is sufficient for safe routing and stop conditions, but venue-specific execution details must be retrieved at runtime.

notes: Research covered authoritative source classes for guest-service workflow, pricing and payment boundaries, and safety or fraud handling before venue workflow claims were drafted.
The resulting spec pack treats ticketing, accessibility, screening, suspicious-ticket handling, and closeout evidence as core lane behavior rather than optional flavor.
